title: Add URL View Parameter for Dictionary vs Migration Navigation
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the application to use a URL view parameter to control whether I see the Dictionary or Migration view, so that navigating between views and using breadcrumbs returns me to the correct context.
description:
The application needed better flow control between the Dictionary and Migration views. Previously, navigation between these two contexts was ambiguous, leading to incorrect page loads and broken breadcrumb behavior.

## Changes

1. **URL view parameter** added to `select.php` — `?view=dictionary` or `?view=migration` routes the user to the correct page.
2. **Default landing page** changed from Migration Scorecard to Dictionary on login.
3. **Dynamic breadcrumb context** on `object.php` — clicking the "Scorecard" breadcrumb navigates back to the select page with the correct `?view=` parameter based on whether the user was viewing a dictionary or migration object.

## Edge Case

A bug was introduced during initial implementation where debug meta fields were printed on the page. This was caused by leftover testing code and was resolved.
acceptance_criteria: Navigating to select.php?view=dictionary loads the Dictionary view
Navigating to select.php?view=migration loads the Migration view
Default landing page after login is the Dictionary view
Clicking 'Scorecard' breadcrumb on object.php navigates back to select.php with the correct view parameter matching the current context
No debug or meta field output appears on any page
ux_changes: Default landing page changed from Migration Scorecard to Dictionary. Breadcrumb on object.php dynamically passes the correct view parameter so clicking 'Scorecard' returns the user to the contextually correct view.
middleware_changes: Server-side logic in select.php reads the `view` URL parameter to determine which page content to render. object.php dynamically sets the view parameter on breadcrumb links based on the current tab context.
implementation_notes: Implementation involved adding a `view` query parameter to select.php and dynamically setting breadcrumb hrefs on object.php. A bug was introduced when debug output of meta fields was left in the code during testing, which was subsequently cleaned up.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the first two URL parameter points quickly, then worked on the dynamic breadcrumb context. A follow-up review caught a bug where debug meta field output was accidentally left on the page; this was traced to leftover testing code and resolved.
